pages.raw_text:
There is cons. lie speculation
among labor men as to who will
represent the American Federation
of Labor in settling the difficulties
existing between the local federal
unions. The logical man to repre-.
sent the federation in the matter
would be Organizer c. o. Young
There is, however, a great deal OF
opposition to Young among mem
bers of Federal union 40. 1922, IT
being stated that he has always
been disposed to give that organi.
cation the short end of the deal.


1t is also stated that Young. as
member of the grievance COM
mittee of the A. F. of L., took the
side of one or the other of the
unions when the local trouble was
up for consideration, and it is
claimed that he should be disquali-
fed from acting in the matter on
account of bias.


1t is also pointed out that Young
has been productive of more dis
sension among local labor unions
than any other one influence, to a
great extent local differences of
any Importance being traced direct
ly to him. Members of Federal
union No. 1922 state they would
rather he never came back to Spo
kane.


When asked if they had prefer
ences for any other representative
of the A. F. of l.. they said anyone
the federation would see fit to send
here or appoint, with the exception
Of ORGANIZER Young, would be per
fectly satisfactory to them.
